decisions.title: Select Distributed Database for K8s-Deploy Architecture
decisions.context: Heavy performance testing underway (ENG-8565) after prototyping (ENG-8564, ENG-7931). Database selection critical for Sift Edge scalability and 'Build the Moat' pillar. Impacts deployment architecture and customer self-hosting capabilities.

arguments.for: Performance tests will provide empirical data on timeseries indexing under real workloads; selecting now enables Q2 Edge alpha deployment
arguments.against: Premature selection risks architectural lock-in before full production load patterns understood; may need additional testing cycles

decisions.title: Migrate Production to Kluctl-Based Deployment Pipeline
decisions.context: Extensive kluctl migration in progress (ENG-7716, 7720-7726). 'Nail the Core' reliability play. Current deployment covers backend, migrations, monitoring stack. Production k8s upgrades staged (ENG-7607-7609). Represents fundamental shift in deployment methodology.

arguments.for: Unified deployment tooling reduces operational complexity; k8s 1.33 upgrade requires clean deployment story; enables faster rollbacks and environment parity
arguments.against: Migration carries risk during critical Q1 close; team learning curve may slow immediate velocity; rollback plan for kluctl itself unclear

decisions.title: Implement Large-File Processing Architecture (Core Algorithm Redesign)
decisions.context: ENG-9000 addresses fundamental data ingestion limitations affecting Explore V2 and Canvas. Channel-oriented processing with commit safety enables 'Embed Deeper' by handling customer files at scale. Blocking advanced visualization features.
decisions.blockedBy: Requires coordination with Kafka partitioning strategy (ENG-9054) and fresh data reads (ENG-8228)

arguments.for: Unblocks customer requests for large file analysis; differentiator for aerospace/automotive customers; directly supports North Star by enabling more decision contexts
arguments.against: Significant architectural change mid-quarter; may destabilize existing ingestion pipeline; requires extensive testing before customer-facing release

decisions.title: Prioritize Production Alerting Completion Over Feature Work
decisions.context: Critical observability gaps: Keycloak registration (ENG-8852), pts-id-service latency (ENG-8981), disk usage (ENG-8982), Kafka lag monitoring (ENG-9053), data-batcher metrics (ENG-9049). Operations blindness risks 'Nail the Core' pillar.

arguments.for: Production incidents currently detected by customers first; Q1 uptime SLA at risk; technical debt compounds with each new service deployment
arguments.against: Alerting work invisible to customers; Q1 feature commitments (Explore V2, Python Rules) may slip; team already stretched across infrastructure work

decisions.title: Approve Data Export File API Architecture (FD-34, FD-40)
decisions.context: Low-level and high-level export interfaces under development. Enables 'Embed Deeper' via programmatic data access. Supports Python Rules and Ask SIFT integration. API design choices impact long-term extensibility.

arguments.for: Unblocks customer automation workflows; enables Python Rules beta; positions Sift as platform vs. tool; competitive feature gap closure
arguments.against: Premature API surface expansion before core stability achieved; export at scale untested; may create support burden

timeAudit.weekSummary: Critical infrastructure week: 60% platform/reliability, 25% product features, 15% planning. Heavy engineering coordination load.
alerts: Zero architecture decision meetings scheduled for distributed DB selection (deadline 3/23)
Alerting sprint at risk: 5 separate alert tickets with no clear completion owner
K8s upgrade window (3/15-3/22) conflicts with Q1 feature freeze dates
Data export API design review not on calendar (needed by 3/14)
